tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function with possible nephrotic features and tubular dysfunction. Blood glucose is normal but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, raising suspicion for underlying renal or hepatic pathology. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
